title: Tiramisu
excerpt: This is a dessert that's already made the day before, and the longer it stands, the better. The Italians say the name means: "Pick me up". After Christmas, we probably all need it. INGREDIENTS 500 ml cream 250 g mascarpone cheese (room temperature) 80 g …
html:
This is a dessert that’s already made the day before, and the longer it stands, the better. The Italians say the name means: “Pick me up”. After Christmas, we probably all need it.

 

INGREDIENTS

  • 500 ml cream
  • 250 g mascarpone cheese (room temperature)
  • 80 g caster sugar
  • 5 ml vanilla essence
  • 1 packet boudoir biscuits (lady finger biscuits)
  • 250 ml strong coffee
  • 80 ml coffee liqueur
  • Cocoa powder
  • 1 flake

METHOD

  1. Whip the cream until it starts to stiffen
  2. Add the vanilla essence and caster sugar and whip to soft peaks
  3. Fold the mascarpone into the cream.
  4. Get a glass dish, because you're now going to pack your biscuits into it
  5. Mix the coffee and the liqueur and quickly dip each biscuit into the mixture
  6. Pack a layer of the biscuits into the bottom of your dish.
  7. Spread half of the cream mixture over the biscuits
  8. Repeat the biscuit layer (remember to dip them in the coffee)
  9. Spread the other half of the cream mixture over.
  10. Using a sieve, sift a thin layer of cocoa powder over. Cover with clingwrap and put in the fridge overnight
  11. Just before serving, crumble the flake over.
